ideal_must_detect.matched_text: Confidential Term Sheet — Proposed acquisition of PT Arunika DPT Teknologi by PT Sawala Dana Nusa Tbk (listed on Bursa Efek Samudra: SDNT), non-binding except as to confidentiality, exclusivity, and governing law (Indonesia).

Overview: Buyer to acquire 80% of the issued shares of Target to integrate a licensed DPT (aset kripto) wallet and a climate-credit marketplace module into Buyer’s fintech ecosystem, subject to approvals from Otoritas Jasa Keuangan Nusantara (OJN) and Badan Pengawas Aset Digital Nusantara (BPADIN), and customary competition clearance.

Consideration: IDR 420,000,000,000 cash at closing plus an earn-out of up to IDR 60,000,000,000 over 24 months tied to (i) monthly active verified DPT wallet users and (ii) volume of verified carbon unit trades on Target’s module, with caps and KPI definitions to be agreed.

Employment and retention: Closing is conditioned on retention agreements with key managers, including Bapak Aditya Wirawan (CTO) and Ibu Nabila Aryasari (Head of Compliance), with 24‑month stay bonuses and IP/assignment covenants; no change-in-control payment will exceed agreed caps without Buyer consent.

MAC/termination: Buyer may terminate if (i) BPADIN suspends or revokes Target’s DPT registration, (ii) any verifier invalidates >10% of listed climate units, or (iii) any additional cyber breach compromises >100,000 data subjects or critical signing keys prior to closing.

Financing and exclusivity: Buyer will fund in cash from internal resources and a committed revolving facility; Seller grants 60 days exclusivity, during which Seller shall not solicit or entertain competing proposals nor disclose deal-stage information beyond clean team protocols.

Confidentiality and data handling: All non-public information is MNPI and will be processed in compliance with UU PDP principles; no KTP numbers or unmasked credential data will be uploaded to the data room, and personal data will be minimized, encrypted at rest, and deleted per the retention schedule after closing lapses or termination.

Closing and timing: Target signing in 45 days and closing within 30 days thereafter subject to CP satisfaction; parties will coordinate board memo workflows for approvals and pre-clear press lines with OJN staff as needed through permitted channels without revealing MNPI.
no KTP numbers or unmasked credential data will be uploaded to the data room

Confidential Term Sheet — Proposed acquisition of PT Arunika DPT Teknologi by PT Sawala Dana Nusa Tbk (listed on Bursa Efek Samudra: SDNT), non-binding except as to confidentiality, exclusivity, and governing law (Indonesia).
Parties and contacts: Buyer — PT Sawala Dana Nusa Tbk (Attn: Bapak Raka Prasetyo, Head of Corporate Development, raka.prasetyo@sdn-tbk.example.id); Seller/Target — PT Arunika DPT Teknologi (Attn: Ibu Dr. Kirana Putri, Legal Counsel, legal@arunika-dpt.example.id).
Overview: Buyer to acquire 80% of the issued shares of Target to integrate a licensed DPT (aset kripto) wallet and a climate-credit marketplace module into Buyer’s fintech ecosystem, subject to approvals from Otoritas Jasa Keuangan Nusantara (OJN) and Badan Pengawas Aset Digital Nusantara (BPADIN), and customary competition clearance.
Consideration: IDR 420,000,000,000 cash at closing plus an earn-out of up to IDR 60,000,000,000 over 24 months tied to (i) monthly active verified DPT wallet users and (ii) volume of verified carbon unit trades on Target’s module, with caps and KPI definitions to be agreed.
Conditions precedent: (a) OJN and BPADIN approvals; (b) confirmation that the 18 May 2026 cyber incident disclosed in diligence (non-public) has been remediated with independent validation and no ongoing lateral movement; (c) no suspension of Target’s BPADIN registration; (d) ESG claim substantiation for climate-credit listings; and (e) customary corporate approvals.
Cyber and compliance diligence: Buyer will complete IT security, AML/CFT for VASP/DPT operations, and climate-claims verification; Buyer’s internal risk scoring has flagged unusual API traffic patterns in Q1 that require deeper log analysis and red-team testing prior to signing.
Governance and information barriers: A clean team will review competitively sensitive DPT user data; information sharing will be limited to aggregated/need-to-know basis under a Chinese wall, and Buyer’s trading desk and treasury must remain segregated from deal communications.
Insider list and blackout: Buyer’s Corporate Secretary will maintain a daftar orang dalam (insider list) including all personnel with access; a blackout window applies from this term sheet until the keterbukaan informasi is publicly posted on BES, and tipping or selective disclosure to any third party is strictly prohibited.
Public disclosure: If signed, a keterbukaan informasi will be filed on BES and via OJN electronic reporting within two business days pursuant to OJN Rule No. 12/2025 on Informasi Material dan Keterbukaan Emiten, with coordinated Q&A and no forward-looking guidance beyond approved messaging.
Employment and retention: Closing is conditioned on retention agreements with key managers, including Bapak Aditya Wirawan (CTO) and Ibu Nabila Aryasari (Head of Compliance), with 24‑month stay bonuses and IP/assignment covenants; no change-in-control payment will exceed agreed caps without Buyer consent.
Representations and warranties: Target to provide reps on (i) title, (ii) no undisclosed data incidents since 18 May 2026, (iii) valid BPADIN registration and sandbox approvals, (iv) veracity of ESG/climate-related disclosures, and (v) no undisclosed material contracts or side letters affecting commercial terms.
MAC/termination: Buyer may terminate if (i) BPADIN suspends or revokes Target’s DPT registration, (ii) any verifier invalidates >10% of listed climate units, or (iii) any additional cyber breach compromises >100,000 data subjects or critical signing keys prior to closing.
Financing and exclusivity: Buyer will fund in cash from internal resources and a committed revolving facility; Seller grants 60 days exclusivity, during which Seller shall not solicit or entertain competing proposals nor disclose deal-stage information beyond clean team protocols.
Confidentiality and data handling: All non-public information is MNPI and will be processed in compliance with UU PDP principles; no KTP numbers or unmasked credential data will be uploaded to the data room, and personal data will be minimized, encrypted at rest, and deleted per the retention schedule after closing lapses or termination.
Operational carve‑out for education: A planned public “Literasi Aset Kripto & Kredit Karbon” campaign may proceed as general education/marketing content and must not include issuer-specific KPIs, pipeline transactions, or forward-looking statements that could be interpreted as selective disclosure.
Closing and timing: Target signing in 45 days and closing within 30 days thereafter subject to CP satisfaction; parties will coordinate board memo workflows for approvals and pre-clear press lines with OJN staff as needed through permitted channels without revealing MNPI.
